title: 3Sum — From O(n³) to O(n²)
description:
Given an array of integers `nums`, return all UNIQUE triples `[a, b, c]` (sorted ascending within each triple) such that a + b + c = 0.

**The point**: brute force is O(n³). Sort + two-pointer gets it to **O(n²)**. The transformation is one of the most-asked patterns in interviews.
hints: Three nested loops give O(n³). Sorting first lets you do something smarter.
Fix the first element nums[i]. Now you need two numbers from the REST that sum to -nums[i].
On a sorted array, two-pointer finds a pair summing to a target in O(n). Combined with the outer loop, that's O(n²) total.
